entryA.symptom: Scan fails with Internal System Error; SHIR logs show 'Module atlas-ingestion-write initialize failed' and 'Could not load file or assembly Microsoft.DataTransfer.Common.RetryPolicies - The system can
entryA.solution: Uninstall current SHIR and install latest version from https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=39717

entryB.symptom: SHIR error: 'Could not load file or assembly System.ValueTuple, Version=4.0.2.0'
entryB.solution: Copy System.ValueTuple.dll from C:\Program Files\Microsoft Integration Runtime\4.0\Gateway\DataScan to C:\Program Files\Microsoft Integration Runtime\4.0\Gateway

entryA.symptom: Scan save fails with error: The version of the linkedservice is not supported — during SHIR scan configuration
entryA.solution: Install private build of SHIR (whitelisted for tenant); uninstall old SHIR with keep-data or use new VM; create new scans with new SHIR. Fix only for Snowflake, MySQL, Postgres. Contact pemar/yazanabu

entryB.symptom: SQL scan via SHIR fails with 'This implementation is not part of the Windows Platform FIPS validated cryptographic algorithms'
entryB.solution: Disable FIPS mode on the self-hosted integration runtime server

entryA.symptom: Azure Synapse Analytics scan fails when using Allowed Trusted Services with Private Purview: trusted service connection insufficient
entryA.solution: Add a Firewall rule in Azure Synapse Network settings to allow Purview IP/VNET. Alternatively use Managed VNet IR with managed PE for Synapse.

entryB.symptom: Test connection passes but scan fails with connection error when using private endpoints or VNets
entryB.solution: Confirm network settings and review NSG rules. Ensure all required ports and endpoints are allowed in NSG.

entryA.symptom: Test connection fails with error '(5522) Test connection failed for lineage scan' or '(5521)' when enabling Lineage extraction for Azure SQL DB scan in Purview
entryA.solution: Set 'Allow Azure services and resources to access this server' to Yes on Azure SQL DB firewall. Lineage extraction does not support servers that disable public access. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com

entryB.symptom: Test connection passes but scan fails with connection error when using private endpoints or VNets
entryB.solution: Confirm network settings and review NSG rules. Ensure all required ports and endpoints are allowed in NSG.

entryA.symptom: Scan fails with 'Name or service not known' or connection errors when using Managed Vnet IR with Private Endpoints. DataScanAgentLinuxEvent logs show HttpRequestException for storage endpoint.
entryA.solution: 1) Use Kusto to extract jobId from ScanningLog, then check DataScanAgentLinuxEvent for networking errors. 2) Ask customer to check PE status in Azure portal (Networking > Private endpoint connections)

entryB.symptom: Test connection passes but scan fails with connection error when using private endpoints or VNets
entryB.solution: Confirm network settings and review NSG rules. Ensure all required ports and endpoints are allowed in NSG.

entryA.symptom: Purview Portal partially loaded with 'Initialization failed' and 'AxiosError: Network Error' when using Private Endpoint. The Microsoft Purview header/chrome loads but content initialization fails.
entryA.solution: Open browser console (Network tab), check failed API requests: If URL contains {accountname}.purview.azure.com -> Account PE or account service issue (verify Account PE is configured for this specific

entryB.symptom: Purview scan fails with 'Internal system error. Please contact support with correlationId' when using private endpoint
entryB.solution: Validate name resolution between SHIR VM and Purview managed blob queue / Event Hubs through port 443 using private IP addresses. Use Test-NetConnection to verify.

entryA.symptom: AWS S3 test connection fails with error about missing external ID in AWS role configuration
entryA.solution: Go to Purview credential page, copy External ID. In AWS IAM, Roles, select role, Trust relationships, edit, add Condition: {StringEquals: {sts:ExternalId: copied-id}}. Save and retest.

entryB.symptom: Amazon S3 scan connection failure: 'Error found with the Role ARN' or role configuration errors
entryB.solution: Verify: 1) AWS role has AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess or minimum S3 read permissions. 2) Trust relationship has correct Microsoft account ID and external ID. 3) Bucket URL uses correct format s3://<BucketNam

entryA.symptom: Cannot access shared data in the target data store after attaching share
entryA.solution: Wait a few minutes for data propagation. Ensure you are using storage API version February 2020 or later. Use latest version of storage SDK, PowerShell, CLI, and Azure Storage Explorer.

entryB.symptom: Can't access shared data in target data store after share attaching succeeds
entryB.solution: Wait a few minutes for data to appear. Ensure using storage SDK/PowerShell/CLI/Azure Storage Explorer with API version February 2020 or later. AzCopy, ADF, and Power BI are not supported for accessing

entryA.symptom: Sensitivity Labels not shown to members of a Unified Group with hidden group membership scoped in label policy
entryA.solution: Disable hidden group membership on the Unified Group, or use a different scoping mechanism (individual users, non-hidden groups, or 'all users').

entryB.symptom: Sensitivity labels not visible to users after creating/publishing label policy in Purview - users cannot see newly published labels in Office apps
entryB.solution: Wait up to 24 hours for label policy propagation. Verify label policy assignment scope includes target users/groups. Check label policy priority order. For 21V, verify bootstrap and endpoint config fo

entryA.symptom: Use-AipServiceKeyVaultKey fails; AIP service cannot access BYOK key in Azure Key Vault even though the key exists
entryA.solution: Verify and grant AIP service the correct Key Vault permissions. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/byok-price-restrictions#enabling-key-authorization-using-power

entryB.symptom: Cannot protect new files with AIP after BYOK key expires in Azure Key Vault — error 'key is not available'
entryB.solution: Generate or import a new key in Azure Key Vault, then use Use-AipServiceKeyVaultKey to register it and Set-AipServiceKeyProperties to set it as Active. Ensure Key Vault keys are renewed before expirat
